full_text: If domain expansions are the pinnacle of Jujutsu Sorcery, then why do some characters have domains but not have things like RCT? What's actually more rare? Let's talk about it. Alright, first of all, shout out to Kido for inspiring this video today, and ultimately he wanted to know what are the hardest techniques to learn in JJK, ranked by their difficulty, and what are the conditions to learning them? And this is a bit tough because it's really nebulous, right? Because you will have characters that have domains that don't have RCT, or have RCT but don't have a domain, right? So why is that the case? And it boils down to a lot of things, namely narrative over everything else, right? But also things just like innate talent, things you might be better at compared to something else, or just things that you literally must be born with, and we'll talk about that. So what I'll do is I'll just rank the rarest things in JJK, and as to why a character might have one or not the other, it really just depends. So of the rarest abilities in JJK, domain expansions are actually the most prominent, where 14 characters by my count have cast a domain expansion, and we are not including Jujutsu Kaisen Mojolo for reference. And 9 of those 14 are sorcerers, so the rest being cursed spirits. So 14 people total have reached the so-called pinnacle of Jujutsu. The next most prominent of the rare abilities is RCT, with 12 sorcerers by my count being capable of this. However, what's interesting is that only 3 of those 12 are capable of outputting it on someone else, which makes outputting RCT one of the rarest things in the series. Now why are some people capable of that and others not? I would just say innate talent, maybe something they're born with, like Shouko, who is just really good at RCT, or Yuta Okotsu. Now it's not to say that someone like Gojo is worse than them because he can't do it, he's just worse at them in that respect. The same way that Steph Curry is the best shooter of all time, but he's not the best defender of all time. People have their strengths and weaknesses, and how you want to gauge those is up to you. Next we have Black Flash, where only 7 characters have hit a Black Flash in the series. 8 if you want to count Yuta Okotsu's anime-only Black Flash. And this is probably the greatest differentiator there is because that's what the series tells us. Sorcerers that have hit Black Flash can far surpass those that haven't. And then finally that brings us to the rarest things in JJK, but these likely don't have anything to do with actual skill and more to do with your technique just having one to begin with. So for starters, we have maximum techniques, which only 3 characters show us in the series. And then on that exact same logic, we have cursed technique reversals. Again, probably not anything to do with skill, assuming you can do reverse curse technique to begin with, but probably your technique just has to have one because only 2 characters in the entire series use a reversal.
